headlines.title: DOJ sets up $1.8B ‘anti-weaponization’ fund after Trump drops IRS lawsuit
headlines.description: The Justice Department on Monday announced that it was establishing a $1.776 billion “Anti-Weaponization Fund” after President Donald Trump moved to dismiss a $10 billion lawsuit against the IRS over his leaked tax returns

headlines.title: Rep. Thomas Massie confronts the full force of Trump’s wrath in a GOP primary
headlines.description: Rep. Thomas Massie, a constant thorn in the side of GOP leaders during his eight terms in Congress, is no stranger to a primary challenge. But this year, President Donald Trump’s fury with the Kentucky libertarian has reached a boiling point.

headlines.title: Pete Hegseth boosts Trump-backed challenger to Rep. Thomas Massie ahead of Kentucky primary
headlines.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth on Monday spoke at a campaign event boosting the President Donald Trump-backed challenger to Rep.

headlines.title: Prediction market ‘watchdog’ launches six-figure ad campaign ahead of Senate hearing
headlines.description: A group that says its mission is to serve as a prediction market “watchdog” is launching a six-figure ad buy this week in the Washington, D.C., media market.

headlines.title: What GOP officials are hopeful — and worried — about six months from the midterms
headlines.description: President Donald Trump isn’t on the ballot this year, but with roughly six months before Election Day, he’ll still likely be the biggest factor in determining the fortunes of the Republican Party

headlines.title: Justice Department sets up 'anti-weaponization fund' after Trump drops IRS lawsuit
headlines.description: The Justice Department announced that President Trump would be dropping his lawsuit against the Internal Revenue Service in "exchange" for the creation of an "anti-weaponization fund." NBC News' Monica Alba reports on the announcement and how the fund could be used.

headlines.title: High-Stakes Primary Elections Test Trump’s Influence of GOP
headlines.description: It is a high stakes political week for President Donald Trump with primary elections that could indicate the future of the Republican party. A key test for Trump’s power is coming up in Kentucky. The Iran war has become a central political issue for Trump and Democrats argue voters will express their frustration at the ballot box. NBC’s Ryan Nobles reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: Hungarian Official Performs Encore of Viral Dance With New Moves
headlines.description: Sunday TODAY’s Willie Geist runs through the Highs and Lows of the week including Hungary’s health minister breaking into an encore performance after his viral dance, a high school hurdler tapping into her gymnastics skills on the track and more!

headlines.title: The war is on hold, but Iran’s internal crackdown is only deepening
headlines.description: While the Iranian regime has been battling its external enemies, Israel and the U.S., it has also been ramping up a deadly crackdown on those seen as the enemy within.

headlines.title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio
headlines.description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

headlines.title: Trump allies and Christian leaders kick off America’s 250th birthday with religious rally on National Mall
headlines.description: Thousands gathered on the National Mall on Sunday for a day of prayer, worship music and patriotic speeches for an event celebrating the nation’s 250th anniversary that supporters hail as a public affirmation of faith in America but critics view as an exclusionary display that blurs the line between religion and politics.

headlines.title: Senate parliamentarian rejects Trump’s ballroom fund in budget bill
headlines.description: A GOP bill seeking $1 billion for the Secret Service to help finance President Donald Trump’s White House ballroom is in jeopardy as it faces pushback from a top Senate official
